steps.title: Track the exposed faces
steps.content: Let $s$ be the side length of each square base. Write the surface area of one prism, then write the surface area after the two prisms are glued together and the glued square faces are no longer exposed.

steps.title: Express surface area formulas
steps.content:
A rectangular prism with square base of side $s$ and height $h$ has surface area $2s^2 + 4sh$.

For each given prism with height 80 cm:
$$K = 2s^2 + 4s(80) = 2s^2 + 320s = 2s(s + 160)$$
When two prisms are glued along a square base, they form a prism of height 160 cm:
$$2s^2 + 4s(160) = 2s^2 + 640s = 2s(s + 320)$$

steps.title: Set up and simplify the ratio equation
steps.content:
The combined surface area is $\frac{33}{17}K$, so:
$$\frac{2s(s + 320)}{2s(s + 160)} = \frac{33}{17}$$
Factor out $2s$ and cancel:
$$\frac{s + 320}{s + 160} = \frac{33}{17}$$

steps.title: Solve for the side length
steps.content:
Cross multiply:
$$17(s + 320) = 33(s + 160)$$
$$17s + 5440 = 33s + 5280$$
$$160 = 16s$$
$$s = 10$$
That matches the correct answer - B is correct.
